message:
feat(playbook): Add --session flag support to single rule add

The --session flag now works for both single and batch add operations,
enabling provenance tracking for individually added rules.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-4zf","title":"[Utils] truncate - Text truncation with ellipsis","description":"","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:14.481959-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:24:20.339309-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:24:03.858274-05:00","comments":[{"id":2,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-4zf","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function truncate(text: string, maxLength: number): string
```

## Implementation Details
- Check if text.length <= maxLength
- If yes, return text unchanged
- If no, return text.slice(0, maxLength - 3) + "..."
- The -3 accounts for ellipsis length
- Total output length will be exactly maxLength

## Why This Matters
Used for preview text in UI output. Keeps display clean and consistent. Example: truncate("This is a very long bullet content", 20) returns "This is a very lo..."

## Edge Cases to Handle
- maxLength < 3 - handle gracefully, maybe return just "..." or throw
- Empty string - return empty
- maxLength exactly text length - return unchanged
- Unicode characters - ensure slice doesn't break surrogate pairs
- Newlines in text - may want to replace with space first","created_at":"2025-12-07T23:15:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-52q","title":"[Cass] safeCassSearch - Search with automatic error recovery","description":"Implement safeCassSearch() wrapper with automatic error recovery.

## Signature
```typescript
async function safeCassSearch(
  query: string, 
  options: CassSearchOptions = {},
  cassPath = "cass"
): Promise<CassHit[]>
```

## Implementation Flow
1. Check cassAvailable() - return [] if cass not installed
2. Check cassNeedsIndex() - rebuild if needed
3. Call cassSearch() with provided options
4. Return results or [] on any failure

## Error Handling
- Never throws exceptions
- Always returns array (empty on error)
- Logs errors at appropriate level
- Performs automatic recovery:
  - Rebuilds index if missing
  - Reduces limit on timeout
  - Retries once after recovery

## Purpose
High-level wrapper for resilient search operations. Use this in:
- reflect command
- recall command  
- remind command
- Any user-facing search functionality

## Usage
```typescript
// Safe to use without try/catch
const hits = await safeCassSearch("error handling", { 
  limit: 10,
  days: 30 
});

if (hits.length === 0) {
  console.log("No results found");
} else {
  processHits(hits);
}
```

## vs cassSearch
- cassSearch: Low-level, may throw, caller handles errors
- safeCassSearch: High-level, never throws, auto-recovery","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:57.822142-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T19:02:10.073029-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T19:02:00.161383-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-5cru","title":"[UX][docs] Fix README drift (config paths, dev command, defaults, binaries)","description":"Problem
- README contains several copy/paste flows that don’t match the current codebase. Examples observed:
  - Repo config path/format: README references `.cass/config.json` but code reads `.cass/config.yaml`.
  - Dev command entrypoint: README references `src/cass-memory.ts` but actual entry is `src/cm.ts`.
  - Default model values drift from runtime defaults.
  - Binary asset naming in install docs may not match build outputs.

What to do
- Do a “docs trust pass”:
  - Ensure all commands, paths, defaults, and examples match the current implementation.
  - Prefer `cm` in examples (but ensure `cass-memory` still works).
  - Keep recovery instructions accurate and safe (no hidden data loss).

Notes
- Do not add any “contributing/contributors” section back into README (project policy).","design":"Implementation sketch
- Add a lightweight “docs audit checklist” to the bead and verify each section against code.
- Optionally add a small test that greps README for known entrypoints/paths to prevent regressions (keep it minimal).","acceptance_criteria":"- README examples are copy/paste correct and match `package.json` scripts + actual CLI flags.
- Config section matches supported formats/precedence.
- Install instructions match actual release asset names (or are updated to be future-proof).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-13T05:19:58.417469-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T16:28:01.373246-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T16:28:01.373246-05:00","labels":["docs","onboarding","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-5cru","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-1ojv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-13T05:20:02.320502-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-5cru","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-czfe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T05:22:17.188722-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-5cru","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-9rni","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T05:22:17.228913-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-5cru","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-2yzr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T05:22:17.268524-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-5cru","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-zdx8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T05:22:17.305186-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-5eh","title":"Phase 1: Foundation + High-ROI Core","description":"# EPIC: Foundation + High-ROI Core

## Purpose
Establish the core infrastructure and implement the highest-ROI features that provide immediate value. This phase creates the skeleton upon which all other features build.

## Background & Reasoning
The ACE (Agentic Context Engineering) framework emphasizes three key innovations:
1. **Generator** - Pre-task context hydration
2. **Reflector** - LLM-powered insight extraction  
3. **Curator** - Deterministic delta merging (NO LLM rewriting)

This phase implements the Generator (context command) and foundational types needed for all subsequent phases.

## Priority Features (from ROI analysis)
- P1: Confidence decay + event timestamps (20× impact)
- P2: Structured JSON context output (10× impact)
- P4: Effective score (4× harmful multiplier) (5× impact)
- P10: State lifecycle draft/active/retired (10× impact)
- P12: Secret sanitization (∞ security-critical)
- P14: Kind enum categorization (3× impact)

## Key Deliverables
1. `cass-memory init` - Initialize configuration and playbook
2. `cass-memory context --json` - Pre-flight task hydration
3. Complete type system with all enhancements
4. cass integration wrapper with error handling
5. Secret sanitization pipeline

## Architecture Decisions
- Use Bun for all JS/TS operations (per AGENTS.md rules)
- Vercel AI SDK for LLM provider abstraction
- Zod for runtime type validation and LLM structured output
- YAML for playbook storage (human-readable, git-friendly)

## Success Criteria
- An agent can run `cass-memory context "task"` and receive relevant bullets
- Bullets have proper decay-adjusted scores
- No secrets leak into stored diary entries or playbook
- Configuration cascades from global to repo-level

## Dependencies
None - this is the foundation phase","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:16:01.370891-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T19:11:39.417385-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T19:11:39.417385-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-931","title":"[Generator] buildContextResult - Assemble final output","description":"Assemble final ContextResult from all gathered components.

SIGNATURE:
function buildContextResult(task: string, rules: ScoredBullet[], antiPatterns: ScoredBullet[], history: CassHit[], warnings: string[]): ContextResult

PURPOSE:
Combine all context components into a structured JSON output optimized for agent consumption. This is what gets returned to the LLM as the 'mission briefing'.

OUTPUT STRUCTURE:
{
  task: string,                    // Original task description
  relevantBullets: Array<{         // Top-scored rules
    id: string,
    content: string,
    category: string,
    type: string,
    maturity: number,
    effectiveScore: number,
    lastHelpful: string,           // Formatted timestamp
    reasoning: string              // Why this rule exists
  }>,
  antiPatterns: Array<{            // Pitfalls to avoid
    id: string,
    content: string,
    type: 'anti-pattern',
    effectiveScore: number
  }>,
  historySnippets: Array<{         // Relevant past sessions
    sessionPath: string,
    agent: string,
    snippet: string,
    relevance: number
  }>,
  deprecatedWarnings: string[],    // Warnings about old patterns
  suggestedCassQueries: string[]   // Human-readable search suggestions
}

TRANSFORMATIONS:
- rules/antiPatterns → Extract only needed fields (strip internal metadata)
- lastHelpful → formatLastHelpful(bullet) for human readability
- reasoning → extractBulletReasoning(bullet) for context
- history → Map to simpler structure (remove line numbers, etc)
- suggestedCassQueries → generateSuggestedQueries(task, keywords)

FIELD DESCRIPTIONS:
- maturity: Confidence score (0-100) based on event count
- effectiveScore: Net helpful score with decay
- lastHelpful: 'Never' or relative time ('2 days ago')
- reasoning: Origin story of the rule

SORTING:
- relevantBullets: Already sorted by finalScore
- antiPatterns: Already sorted by finalScore
- historySnippets: Already sorted by relevance

SIZE LIMITS:
- relevantBullets: config.maxBulletsInContext (default 10)
- antiPatterns: config.maxBulletsInContext (default 10)
- historySnippets: config.maxHistorySnippets (default 10)

This structure is designed to be directly embeddable in LLM context.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:59.484609-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:06:29.944025-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:06:23.733329-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-94c5","title":"[Tracking] Workspace-specific processed log","description":"Purpose: Separate processed session logs per workspace for workspace-scoped reflection

File path pattern: ~/.cass-memory/reflections/ws-{hash}.processed.log

Hash generation:
- hash = first 8 characters of SHA256(workspace absolute path)
- Example: /Users/dev/myapp → SHA256 → a1b2c3d4 → ws-a1b2c3d4.processed.log

This keeps workspace sessions separate from global sessions.

Implementation:
```typescript
function getWorkspaceLogPath(workspacePath: string): string {
  const hash = crypto.createHash('sha256')
    .update(workspacePath)
    .digest('hex')
    .substring(0, 8);
  return path.join(
    os.homedir(),
    '.cass-memory',
    'reflections',
    `ws-${hash}.processed.log`
  );
}
```

Format: Same TSV format as global.processed.log
Benefits:
- Workspace-specific reflection history
- Independent processing of workspace vs global sessions
- Easy cleanup when workspace deleted","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:51:00.368578-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T22:07:14.675289-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T22:07:14.675289-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-95b","title":"[Utils] now - Current ISO 8601 timestamp","description":"","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:01.127777-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:24:20.345943-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:24:03.877633-05:00","comments":[{"id":6,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-95b","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function now(): string
```

## Implementation Details
- Returns new Date().toISOString()
- Format: "2024-01-15T14:30:00.000Z"
- Always UTC timezone
- ISO 8601 standard format

## Why This Matters
Consistent timestamp format throughout system. Used for createdAt, updatedAt, lastUsedAt fields. ISO 8601 is sortable lexicographically and parseable by all date libraries.

## Edge Cases to Handle
- None - Date.toISOString() is very robust
- Milliseconds always included (3 decimal places)
- Always ends with Z (UTC indicator)","created_at":"2025-12-07T23:15:50Z"}]}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-9hf1","title":"[UX][safety] Add dry-run/preview mode for destructive operations","description":"Problem
- Even with confirmations, users need to see exactly what will change before committing to a destructive action.

What to do
- Add a `--dry-run` (or `--preview`) mode to destructive paths:
  - playbook remove --hard
  - undo --hard
  - any future “reset config / rebuild” operations
- The preview should include:
  - bullet id + a short content preview
  - target file path (global vs repo)
  - the exact flags/command to apply for real

Notes
- Preview output should be safe for JSON mode as well (structured plan object).","design":"Implementation sketch
- Implement preview generation first, then wire into confirmation flows.
- Reuse a shared “plan” structure so `cm doctor --dry-run` and destructive commands can share patterns.","acceptance_criteria":"- Destructive commands support `--dry-run` and do not change files when set.
- Preview output is explicit about which file(s) would be modified.
- JSON mode returns a structured plan that is easy to consume.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-13T05:19:08.089348-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T17:35:10.790817-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T17:35:10.790817-05:00","labels":["cli","safety","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-9hf1","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-jfc5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-13T05:19:13.089389-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-9jgu","title":"[E2E] Team Workflow - Repo Setup and Collaboration","description":"## Target: E2E test simulating team usage

### Scenario: Team Setting Up Shared Rules

```
Lead creates git repo
Lead runs init --repo
Lead adds project rules to .cass/playbook.yaml
Lead commits and pushes
Dev clones repo
Dev runs cm doctor (sees repo rules)
Dev gets context (uses merged rules)
```

### Test Script:

```typescript
describe('E2E: Team Workflow', () => {
  let repoDir: string;
  let cloneDir: string;

  it('step 1: lead creates repo with .cass/', async () => {
    repoDir = await createTempGitRepo();
    log('Created repo:', repoDir);
    
    const result = await runCLI(['init', '--repo', '--json'], { cwd: repoDir });
    log('Init result:', result.stdout);
    
    expect(result.exitCode).toBe(0);
    
    // Add custom rule
    await runCLI(['playbook', '--add', 'Always use TypeScript strict mode', '--scope', 'project:test'], { cwd: repoDir });
    log('Added project rule');
    
    // Commit
    await execAsync('git add .cass/ && git commit -m "Add cass config"', { cwd: repoDir });
    log('Committed .cass/');
  });

  it('step 2: dev clones and sees rules', async () => {
    cloneDir = await cloneRepo(repoDir);
    log('Cloned to:', cloneDir);
    
    const doctor = await runCLI(['doctor', '--json'], { cwd: cloneDir });
    log('Doctor result:', doctor.stdout);
    
    const output = JSON.parse(doctor.stdout);
    expect(output.repoPlaybook).toBe(true);
  });

  it('step 3: context includes repo rules', async () => {
    const context = await runCLI(['context', 'typescript config', '--json'], { cwd: cloneDir });
    log('Context result:', context.stdout);
    
    const output = JSON.parse(context.stdout);
    expect(output.bullets.some(b => b.content.includes('TypeScript strict'))).toBe(true);
  });
});
```

### Logging:
- Full git command output
- File contents at each step
- Merge resolution details","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:31:12.508002-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T20:41:37.199656-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T20:41:37.199656-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-9jgu","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-uufj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:32:38.455069-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-9jgu","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-zc1d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:32:39.028162-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-9jgu","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-ke2x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:33:05.638636-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-9n7f","title":"[Tracking] ProcessedEntry type - Session processing record","description":"Type definition:
```typescript
interface ProcessedEntry {
  id: string;              // Unique entry ID (diary-{uuid})
  sessionPath: string;     // Original session path (absolute or provider-specific)
  processedAt: string;     // ISO 8601 timestamp
  diaryId?: string;        // Generated diary ID (if diary was created)
  deltasProposed: number;  // How many deltas reflector proposed
  deltasApplied: number;   // How many passed validation + curation
}
```

Field details:
- id: Unique identifier for this processing event
- sessionPath: Original session file/database path
  * Claude: ~/.claude/projects/{name}/session-{id}.jsonl
  * Cursor: ~/.cursor/state.vscdb:session-{id}
  * Aider: .aider.input.history
- processedAt: When reflection happened (for audit trail)
- diaryId: Optional diary entry ID (may not create diary if no insights)
- deltasProposed: LLM suggested this many changes
- deltasApplied: Only this many made it through validation

Used to populate ProcessedLog entries and track reflection effectiveness.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:50:41.792015-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T21:00:31.31293-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T21:00:29.263047-05:00"}
